description_long: This object is a dark, amphibian-like airplane with a smooth, streamlined fuselage and a long, tapering tail. The body is rounded and bulbous at the front, with a subtle dorsal ridge running along the back. The surface appears glossy and hard, with a deep brown coloration throughout. The wings are not visibly distinct from the main body, blending seamlessly into the fuselage, and the landing gear is not clearly defined. The overall form is organic, resembling a blend between a salamander and an aircraft, with four protruding limbs that mimic legs rather than traditional airplane wings or landing gear. There are no visible windows, engines, or cockpit features, giving it a unique, almost creature-like appearance.
description: A glossy, dark brown airplane with a streamlined, organic body and a long, tapering tail, featuring limb-like protrusions instead of traditional wings or landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated, smooth body, long tail, and two limb-like protrusions extending downward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded, bulbous nose and symmetrical limb placement, with the tail receding into the background.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, displaying the streamlined body and tail, with the limb-like features visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the full length of the body and tail, as well as the symmetrical arrangement of the limbs.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: No distinct wings; limb-like protrusions extend from the fuselage, blending into the body with no visible dihedral or winglets.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present on the fuselage or limbs.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a long, tapering extension; no visible vertical or horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No visible landing gear; limb-like structures may serve as supports.
attribute_cockpit_placement: No visible cockpit or canopy; no windows or openings apparent on the fuselage.
